name: El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park
name_original: El Rugidero – circular por el Parque Natural de Cornalvo
region_intro: Hiking around Mirandilla, located near Mérida in Spain, offers a blend of natural beauty and historical exploration. The region is characterized by its significant Roman heritage, including the ancient Proserpina Reservoir and the Cornalvo Natural Park, which features a historic Roman dam. Hikers can explore oak dehesas and varied terrain around these water bodies, which are integral to the landscape. The area provides a diverse environment for outdoor activities, from gentle reservoir loops to more challenging paths within the natural park. Best hiking trails around Mirandilla The most popular hiking route is Return to Proserpina Reservoir, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 29 minutes to complete. This route explores the surroundings of the historic Proserpina Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cornalvo Reservoir Loop, an easy 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This trail offers views of the Cornalvo Reservoir and the surrounding natural park. Local hikers also love the El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of Cornalvo Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 14 minutes. Hiking around Mirandilla is defined by ancient Roman reservoirs, oak dehesas, and natural parkland. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Mirandilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Mirandilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Mirandilla?
A: Mirandilla offers a wide selection of routes, with nearly 40 hiking trails to explore. These range from easy strolls around reservoirs to more challenging treks through natural parks, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for hiking trails in Mirandilla?
A: The trails around Mirandilla cater to all experience levels. You'll find 12 easy routes, 22 moderate options, and 5 more difficult trails, ensuring there's a suitable path for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mirandilla?
A: Yes, Mirandilla features several excellent circular routes. For an easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. If you're looking for a more challenging circular trail, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991628272/el-rugidero-circular-by-the-cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero – circular by the Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer, immersive experience.

Q: What kind of landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Mirandilla?
A: Hiking in Mirandilla offers a unique blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3350184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Roman Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2990429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Dam&lt;/a&gt; within the Cornalvo Natural Park. The proximity to Mérida also means you can visit significant Roman archaeological sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of Diana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Mirandilla suitable for families?
A: Many trails around Mirandilla are suitable for families, especially those around the reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359872190/return-to-proserpina-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Proserpina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, shorter option that allows families to enjoy the natural surroundings and historical context without excessive difficulty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mirandilla?
A: The hiking trails in Mirandilla are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the blend of natural beauty with historical Roman sites, the well-maintained paths, and the peaceful atmosphere of the reservoir and natural park areas.

Q: When is the best time to go hiking in Mirandilla?
A: Mirandilla is an excellent destination for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures ideal for hiking and exploring the natural parks and historical sites. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable for shorter, easier routes, especially around the reservoirs.

Q: Are there any trails that feature the Roman aqueducts?
A: Yes, the region is rich in Roman heritage. While specific hiking trails might not directly follow the entire aqueduct, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, which once supplied water from the Proserpina reservoir to Mérida, is a prominent landmark in the area and can be explored in conjunction with nearby routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Mirandilla hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Mirandilla is diverse, characterized by oak dehesas, reservoir shores, and natural parkland. You'll encounter mostly gentle paths around the reservoirs, while trails within the Cornalvo Natural Park may include more varied and undulating ground, offering a mix of easy walks and more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there opportunities for exploring natural parks in Mirandilla?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359873627/cornalvo-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a significant feature of the region, offering extensive hiking opportunities. It's home to the historic Roman Cornalvo Dam and provides a rich environment for nature lovers to explore oak forests and diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the reservoirs?
A: Many trails in Mirandilla are centered around its impressive reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2359872190/return-to-proserpina-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return to Proserpina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991631021/cornalvo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; are prime examples, offering beautiful views of these historic water bodies and their surrounding natural areas.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Mirandilla?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, areas around popular spots like the Proserpina Reservoir and Cornalvo Natural Park generally offer designated parking facilities to accommodate hikers. It's advisable to check specific route information on komoot for the most accurate parking recommendations.
tips: El Rugidero is a popular circular route in the Cornalvo Natural Park. Approximately 19.8 km. It is an excellent option for hiking lovers looking for a challenge and enjoying nature.
